prompt: Explain how gorilla/mux builds a router, records route matchers, matches an HTTP request, and invokes middleware or the selected handler.

expected_symbols.name: Router
expected_symbols.why: Owns route list, matching options, middleware, and request dispatch.

expected_symbols.name: Route
expected_symbols.why: Owns per-route matchers and handler metadata.

expected_symbols.name: RouteMatch
expected_symbols.why: Carries the matching route, handler, and variables.

expected_symbols.name: newRouteRegexp
expected_symbols.why: Compiles route templates into matching expressions.

expected_claims.text: NewRouter constructs a Router that owns routes and middleware.
Routes record matchers and handlers used during request matching.
RouteMatch carries the selected route, handler, and variables across matching.
Route template parsing and regular expression compilation happen during route setup through addRegexpMatcher and newRouteRegexp.

forbidden_claims.text: Route regular expressions are compiled for every request instead of during route setup.
Middleware is unrelated to request dispatch in the router.
